name: Turkish Menemen
steps: Saute the bell pepper in the oil until softened.
Add the tomatoes and simmer 8-10 minutes until thickened.
Pour in the beaten eggs and stir gently until just set, like a soft scramble.
steps_detailed: Saute the bell pepper in the oil over medium heat, 5-6 minutes, until softened.
Add the diced tomatoes and paprika, and simmer 10-12 minutes until thickened.
Pour in the beaten eggs and stir gently over low heat until just set and creamy, not fully scrambled.

Scatter with the feta and serve immediately with bread - menemen is a beloved Turkish breakfast, distinct from shakshuka in that the eggs are stirred through rather than left as whole poached pockets.

name: Turkish Simit with Cheese
steps: Make a simple dough from the flour and warm water, shaping into a ring.
Coat generously in the sesame seeds and bake at 400F for 18-22 minutes until golden.
Serve with the feta.

ingredients_full.note: for the glaze, mixed with water

steps_detailed: Make a soft, elastic dough from the flour, a pinch of salt, and warm water. Knead well and rest 20-30 minutes.
Shape into a ring and brush with a thin honey-water glaze, then coat generously in the sesame seeds.
Bake at 400F (200C) for 20-25 minutes until deeply golden and crisp on the outside.
Serve with the feta and tea - simit, a sesame-crusted bread ring, is sold on every street corner in Turkey, especially for breakfast.
A proper Turkish breakfast spread is built around several small plates together - olives alongside the simit and cheese are as standard as the bread itself, not a garnish.

name: Turkish Kaymak with Honey

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps: Spoon the yogurt into a bowl.
Drizzle with the honey and top with the walnuts.
Serve with warm bread.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps_detailed: Spoon the thick yogurt (standing in for kaymak, a rich clotted cream) into a bowl.
Drizzle generously with the honey and top with the walnuts.
Serve with warm bread - kaymak and honey is a classic, indulgent Turkish breakfast pairing.

name: Turkish Lahmacun
steps: Make a simple dough from the flour and water, and roll out thin.
Mix the beef with the tomatoes and onion, and spread thinly over the dough.
Bake at 450F for 8-10 minutes until the edges are crisp.
steps_detailed: Make a soft dough from the flour, a pinch of salt, and water. Roll out very thin.
Mix the beef with the tomatoes, onion, garlic, and paprika, and spread in a very thin, even layer over the dough, right to the edges.
Bake at 450F (230C) for 8-12 minutes until the crust is crisp and the meat is cooked through.
Finish with a squeeze of lime, roll up, and eat - lahmacun, sometimes called Turkish pizza, is a beloved street food.

name: Turkish Doner-Style Chicken
steps: Marinate the chicken with the paprika, garlic, and lime juice for at least 20 minutes.
Pan-fry over high heat, 5-6 minutes, until well browned and cooked through.
Serve with flatbread and a yogurt sauce.
steps_detailed: Marinate the thinly sliced chicken with the paprika, garlic, lime juice, oil, and cinnamon for at least 30 minutes, or overnight.
Cook over high heat in a wide skillet, 6-8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until deeply browned and slightly crisp at the edges, mimicking the charred exterior of a doner spit.
Serve with warm flatbread, pickles, and a yogurt sauce - doner kebab is one of Turkey's most famous culinary exports.

name: Turkish Manti

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps: Make a simple dough from the flour and water, roll thin, and cut into small squares.
Fill each square with a small amount of the beef and pinch closed into tiny dumplings.
Boil until they float, about 8-10 minutes, and serve topped with the yogurt mixed with garlic.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps_detailed: Make a firm, smooth dough from the flour, a pinch of salt, and water, and roll out as thin as possible.
Cut into small squares, place a tiny amount of the seasoned beef in the center of each, and pinch the corners together to seal into small dumplings.
Boil in batches for 8-12 minutes until they float and are cooked through.
Serve topped with the garlic yogurt, a drizzle of melted butter, and a dusting of paprika - manti, Turkey's tiny dumplings, are famously labor-intensive to make by hand.

name: Turkish Pide
steps: Make a soft dough from the flour and warm water, and shape into a long boat shape with raised edges.
Top with the beef and feta.
Bake at 450F for 12-15 minutes, cracking the egg on top for the last 3 minutes.
steps_detailed: Make a soft, elastic dough from the flour, a pinch of salt, and warm water. Rest 20-30 minutes.
Shape into a long, boat-like oval with raised, pinched edges to hold the filling.
Top with the beef, onion, and feta.
Bake at 450F (230C) for 12-16 minutes, cracking the egg into the center for the final 3-4 minutes of baking.
Brush the crust with melted butter as it comes out - pide, Turkey's answer to a boat-shaped flatbread pizza, comes in countless topping variations.

name: Turkish Adana Kebab
steps: Mix the lamb with the grated onion, paprika, and jalapeno.
Shape into flattened logs and grill or pan-fry, 4-5 minutes per side, until cooked through.
Serve with rice or flatbread.
steps_detailed: Mix the lamb thoroughly with the grated onion, paprika, jalapeno, and garlic until well combined and slightly sticky.
Shape into flattened logs around skewers if you have them.
Grill or pan-fry in the oil, 4-6 minutes per side, until well charred and cooked through.
Serve with rice or flatbread - Adana kebab, named for the southern Turkish city, is prized for its bold spice and char.

name: Turkish Iskender Kebab

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw
365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps: Pan-fry the beef until well browned and cooked through.
Simmer the tomatoes into a thick sauce and pour over the beef.
Top with the yogurt and melted butter, and serve over flatbread.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw
365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps_detailed: Pan-fry the thinly sliced beef with the garlic over high heat, 5-7 minutes, until well browned.
Simmer the diced tomatoes separately, 8-10 minutes, until thickened into a rich sauce, then pour over the beef.
Top with the yogurt and melted browned butter, and serve over pieces of flatbread that soak up the sauce - Iskender kebab, named for its creator in Bursa, is one of Turkey's most indulgent dishes.

name: Turkish Stuffed Peppers (Biber Dolmasi)
steps: Mix the raw rice with the beef and onion.
Pack into the peppers and simmer in the tomatoes, covered, 35-40 minutes.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Raisins, dark, seedless (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Mix the raw rice with the beef, onion, cinnamon, pistachios, and raisins - the sweet-savory combination of dried fruit and nuts through the meat filling is characteristic of Turkish dolma specifically, distinct from plainer meat-and-rice fillings elsewhere in the region.
Pack loosely into the halved peppers, leaving room for the rice to expand.
Arrange in a pot, pour the tomatoes and a splash of water over, cover, and simmer 35-40 minutes until the peppers are tender and the rice inside is fully cooked.

name: Turkish Kofte
steps: Mix the beef with the grated onion, flour, and cinnamon.
Shape into small oval patties and grill or pan-fry, 4-5 minutes per side, until well browned and cooked through.
Serve with rice and a salad.
steps_detailed: Mix the beef thoroughly with the grated onion, flour, cinnamon, garlic, and paprika until well combined.
Shape into small oval patties.
Grill or pan-fry, 4-6 minutes per side, until well browned and cooked through.
Serve with rice and a simple salad - kofte, in countless regional and family variations, is one of Turkey's most everyday comfort dishes.

name: Turkish Cacik

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps: Mix the yogurt with the cucumber and garlic.
Season with salt and chill before serving.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

ingredients_full.note: cup, chopped, as a mint stand-in

steps_detailed: Mix the yogurt with the finely diced cucumber, garlic, oil, and herbs.
Season with salt and chill at least 15 minutes before serving - cacik, a cooling yogurt-cucumber dish, is a Turkish table staple.

name: Turkish Fried Eggplant with Yogurt

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps: Fry the eggplant slices in the oil until golden, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Serve topped with the yogurt.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps_detailed: Fry the eggplant slices in the oil over medium heat, 3-5 minutes per side, until deeply golden and soft.
Mix the yogurt with the garlic.
Serve the warm eggplant topped with the garlic yogurt - a simple, popular Turkish meze.

name: Turkish Roasted Chickpeas (Leblebi)

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

steps: Pat the chickpeas dry and toss with the oil and paprika.
Roast at 425F for 20-25 minutes, shaking occasionally, until crisp.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

steps_detailed: Pat the chickpeas very dry with a towel.
Toss with the oil and paprika.
Roast at 425F (220C) for 22-28 minutes, shaking the pan occasionally, until deeply golden and crisp - leblebi, roasted chickpeas, are a classic Turkish snack, often paired with dried fruit and nuts.

name: Turkish Borek
steps: Layer the phyllo with the melted butter.
Mix the feta with the egg and spread between layers, then roll or fold into a log or triangles.
Bake at 375F for 20-25 minutes until deeply golden.
steps_detailed: Layer sheets of phyllo, brushing each with melted butter.
Mix the feta with the beaten egg, spread over the layered phyllo, and roll tightly into a log, or fold into triangles.
Brush the top with more butter and scatter with sesame seeds.
Bake at 375F (190C) for 22-28 minutes until deeply golden and flaky - borek, in its many shapes and fillings, is a beloved Turkish savory pastry.

name: Turkish Baklava
steps: Layer the phyllo with the melted butter, scattering the chopped walnuts between layers.
Bake at 350F for 25-30 minutes until deeply golden.
Pour the honey over while still warm.
steps_detailed: Layer sheets of phyllo in a small buttered dish, brushing each layer with melted butter, and scattering the chopped walnuts (mixed with the cinnamon) between every few layers.
Cut into diamond or square shapes before baking.
Bake at 350F (175C) for 28-35 minutes until deeply golden and crisp.

Pour the honey evenly over the hot baklava as soon as it comes out of the oven, letting it soak in as it cools - baklava, though claimed by several countries, is deeply woven into Turkish and former Ottoman dessert tradition.

name: Turkish Apricot Dessert (Kayisi Tatlisi)
steps: Simmer the dried apricots in water until plump and soft, about 15 minutes.
Fill each with a little of the milk mixed with sugar.
Top with the walnuts and chill before serving.
steps_detailed: Simmer the dried apricots in water to cover for 15-20 minutes until plump and very soft, then drain and let cool slightly.
Warm the milk with the sugar and rosewater until slightly thickened, about 3-4 minutes.
Split each apricot open and fill with a spoonful of the sweetened milk.
Top with the chopped walnuts and chill before serving - a beloved Turkish dessert built on the country's famous dried apricots.

name: Turkish Delight (Lokum)
steps: Whisk the cornstarch with 3/4 cup water until smooth, then simmer, stirring constantly, until very thick, about 10 minutes.
Stir in the sugar and rosewater, and cook 5 more minutes.
Fold in the pistachios, pour into a small dish, and chill until firm before cutting into cubes.
steps_detailed: Whisk the cornstarch with 3/4 cup water until completely smooth.
Simmer over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, 10-12 minutes until very thick and glossy.
Stir in the sugar, rosewater, and lime juice, and cook 5-6 more minutes, stirring, until it pulls together into a thick, gel-like mass.
Fold in the chopped pistachios, pour into a small lined dish, smoothing the top, and chill at least 2-3 hours until fully set.
Cut into cubes and dust with a little extra cornstarch or powdered sugar if desired - lokum, known internationally as Turkish delight, is one of Turkey's most famous sweets.

name: Turkish Garlic Yogurt Sauce

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps: Whisk the yogurt, garlic, and lime juice together.
Season with salt and serve alongside kebabs or manti.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps_detailed: Whisk the yogurt, garlic, lime juice, and oil together until smooth.
Season with salt and chill briefly - this garlic yogurt is essential alongside Turkish manti and many kebabs.

name: Turkish Ezme (Spicy Tomato Relish)
steps: Combine the tomatoes, onion, jalapeno, and lime juice.
Season with salt and serve alongside grilled meats.
steps_detailed: Combine the tomatoes, onion, jalapeno, paprika, lime juice, and oil, mashing slightly to release the juices.
Season with salt and let sit 10-15 minutes before serving - ezme is a fiery, tangy relish served alongside kebabs across Turkey.
